prompt:
Read the following reasoning trace carefully. Your task is to analyze whether
it contains any cognitive biases.

Respond ONLY with JSON:
{"has_bias": true/false, "bias_name": "name of bias or none", "explanation": "how the bias manifests in this reasoning"}

--- Reasoning Trace ---
The candidate has a Stanford CS degree, four years at Google, and strong
system design interview scores. That's exactly the profile we're looking for.
I'm going to recommend a strong hire. His behavioral interview was a bit
mixed — the interviewer noted he struggled to give examples of cross-team
collaboration and his conflict resolution answer was vague. But the technical
bar is met and exceeded, and we've been trying to fill this senior role for
three months. A Stanford-to-Google pipeline with strong system design skills
is rare in our applicant pool. I don't want to overthink this one.
--- End Trace ---

parsed.explanation: The evaluator's strong positive impression from technical credentials (Stanford degree, Google tenure, system design skills) is causing them to overlook and dismiss legitimate behavioral concerns (poor cross-team collaboration examples, vague conflict resolution answers). The reasoning explicitly acknowledges these red flags but then discounts them ('But the technical bar is met...'), allowing the halo of technical excellence to overshadow behavioral fit—which is relevant to senior role performance. The closing statement 'I don't want to overthink this one' reveals awareness of the conflicting signals but an unwillingness to integrate them properly. Secondary influences: scarcity bias (rare profile, 3-month vacancy) and confirmation bias (focusing on reasons to hire while minimizing contradictory evidence).
